Title: Implement SME commission workflow (Opp checkbox + % field + tooltip; manual rate entry)

Acceptance Criteria: - Opportunity details includes SME boolean + SME percent.; - When SME is checked and user adds a product, a tooltip/inline helper text appears explaining the required rate adjustment.; - User can manually override the commission rate (e.g., change 16% to 8%).; - Core commission formulas are unchanged (manual adjustment is the mechanism).

Title: Template evolution: implement 'New Fields' + 'Exclude' tabs and auto-promote mapped fields
Description: In deposit field-mapping, separate unmapped deposit columns into 'New Fields' (unmapped fields that have values) vs 'Exclude' (blank/ignored). When users map new fields and save, those new mappings are added to the vendor template so subsequent uploads treat them as template fields.
Acceptance Criteria: - Mapping UI has 3 groups/tabs: Template Fields, New Fields, Exclude.; - New Fields shows only deposit columns that have at least one non-null value and are not yet in the template.; - Exclude contains deposit columns that are blank/ignored.; - After user maps New Fields + clicks Save, the template is updated; re-opening mapping shows those fields under Template Fields (and New Fields shrinks accordingly).; - No commission calculations change due to these metadata-only fields.
Notes/Dependencies: Dependency: vendor template data model must support adding new template fields at runtime.; Assumption: excluded fields are not persisted beyond the template unless needed for auditing.
Status Update Notes: New Fields/Exclude workflow and template promotion behavior are implemented in the mapping flow.

Acceptance Criteria: - Compare files and document columns that differ.; - Create/adjust Colt Tower template so both file variants map cleanly.; - Validate that new/different columns land in New Fields/Exclude as expected.; - Upload both variants successfully without breaking matching.
Notes/Dependencies: Dependency: needs deposit files available in test env.

Title: Many-to-one bundle conversion workflow: replace bundled product/schedule with individual products + schedule series
Description: When a deposit line item maps to multiple schedules with different commission rates, block the match and offer a remediation workflow that replaces the bundled product/schedule setup with separate products and schedule series built from the deposit data.
Acceptance Criteria: Auto-validate rate conflict before match; plain-language prompt explains why bundle cannot be matched; on confirm, create separate opportunity products from deposit line data; create schedule series that mirrors original bundle timeline; remove/deactivate original bundled product + schedules; write audit trail.
Notes/Dependencies: Expands MTG-0303-014. ASSUMED: original bundle removal should be soft-delete/audited, not hard delete.
Status Update Notes: Bundle rip-and-replace workflow is implemented with dedicated route/tests covering mixed-rate replacement behavior.
